summary: The parallel/test-debugger-exec test timed out after 15,000 ms waiting for the debugger initial break event. The inspector attached successfully but the expected '/break (?:on start )?in/i' pattern was never observed within the timeout window, causing the test to fail and make to exit with error code 1.
root_cause: The test parallel/test-debugger-exec timed out waiting for the debugger initial break signal (pattern /break (?:on start )?in/i) within the 15000ms timeout. The debugger inspector connected and attached successfully, but the expected break event was not received in time, indicating a timing/flakiness issue in the debugger test on macOS CI.

evidence.quote: Error: Timeout (15000) while waiting for /break (?:on start )?in/i
evidence.reason: This is the direct error thrown by the test harness, showing the test timed out waiting for the initial debugger break pattern within 15 seconds.
The debugger did successfully attach, confirming this is not a connection failure but a timing issue where the break event was not emitted or observed within the timeout.
The inspector connection was established successfully, ruling out a network or port binding issue as the root cause.
Only one test failed out of 5175, consistent with an isolated flaky timing failure rather than a systemic build or configuration problem.
The harness was configured with flaky test retry logic, suggesting this test has known flakiness concerns, supporting a timing/flakiness classification.
The make target failed as a direct downstream consequence of the single test timeout, propagating to the CI job exit.

suggested_fix: Investigate whether the parallel/test-debugger-exec test is a known flaky test on macOS and consider increasing the timeout beyond 15000ms in test/common/debugger.js, or re-run the CI job to confirm whether this is a transient flake. Review recent changes to inspector/debugger behavior that may have altered initial break timing on macOS.
